title: Testing DSPs on Mobile Devices
text: Testing DSPs on Mobile Devices During development you can test the user interface on mobile devices by scanning a QR code for the URL of the dynamic server page. Ensure that your development web server is accessible to the mobile device, for example via a WiFi network. Edit the logical TEST_COMMAND_CPT_WEB to replace localhost with the actual host name. You can specify a network address ( AMS12345D1.myorg.local ) or an IP number ( 123.456.789.00 ) instead. For example: ;ide.asn [LOGICALS] TEST_COMMAND_CPT_WEB = http://AMS093606D2:%TomcatPort/uniface/wrd/%CptName Copy Generate a QR code for the main DSP of your mobile app. Open the DSP and choose Actions >Show QR Code . Scan the QR code with your phone or tablet to run the server page in your device's web browser. Alternatively, you can click the QR code to open it in a browser on your desktop machine, or press Ctrl+D . If you experience problems in displaying the page, ensure that the Uniface Router and the Tomcat web service are running. In the Windows Services, look for the services listed under Uniface, such as Uniface XXX Development Tomcat and Uniface XXX Development URouter You can restart the services here.
